zh.lifeSummary: 當James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond於1504年出生在愛爾蘭蒙斯特時，他的父親Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond40歲，母親Móur O'Brien34歲。他於1529年與Joan Roche在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯登波結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1542年在愛爾蘭庫迪 度布林被列為lord high treasurer of ireland。他去世於1558年10月27日在愛爾蘭庫迪 利默里克阿斯可亞頓享年54歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭蒙斯特迪 本 利默里克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond于1504年出生在爱尔兰蒙斯特时，他的父亲Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond40岁，母亲Móur O'Brien34岁。他于1529年与Joan Roche在爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯登波结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1542年在爱尔兰庫迪 度布林被列为lord high treasurer of ireland。他去世于1558年10月27日在爱尔兰庫迪 利默里克阿斯可亞頓享年54岁，埋葬在爱尔兰蒙斯特迪 本 利默里克。

nameMeanings.name: Fitzgerald,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Jarrell
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex; of Norman origin): variant of Jarrold, itself a variant of Gerald .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: FitzJohn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name John , ‘son of John’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

variants.name: Fidgeon
meanings: 1  English: (i) variant of   Vivian  .  Compare  Videan  . (ii) variant of   Fitzjohn  .
2 Irish: reduced variant of Mag Guagáin, see McGuigan .

historicEvents.description: Henry VIII dies, succeeded by the boy king Edward VI. England and Ireland were ruled by the senior nobility of England.

en.lifeSummary: When James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond was born in 1504, in Munster, Ireland, his father, Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond, was 40 and his mother, Móur O'Brien, was 34. He married Joan Roche in 1529, in Dunboyne, County Meath, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1542, his occupation is listed as lord high treasurer of ireland in County Dublin, Ireland. He died on 27 October 1558, in Askeaton, County Limerick, Ireland, at the age of 54, and was buried in Diocese of Limerick, Munster, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond est né en 1504, à Munster, Irlande, son père, Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Móur O'Brien, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Joan Roche en 1529, à Dunboyne, County Meath, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1542, sa profession de son indiquée est lord high treasurer of ireland à County Dublin, Irlande. Il est décédé le 27 octobre 1558, à Askeaton, County Limerick, Irlande, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Diocese of Limerick, Munster, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond 1504 in Munster, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond 40 und seine Mutter Móur O'Brien 34. Er hat 1529 in Dunboyne, County Meath, Irland Joan Roche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1542 ist sein Beruf als lord high treasurer of ireland in County Dublin, Irland aufgeführt. Er starb am 27. Oktober 1558 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Askeaton, County Limerick, Irland und wurde in Diocese of Limerick, Munster, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond è nato il nel 1504 nel Munster, Irlanda, il suo padre, Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Móur O'Brien, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Joan Roche nel 1529, nel Dunboyne, County Meath, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1542, la sua occupazione è indicata come lord high treasurer of ireland nel County Dublin, Irlanda. Morì il 27 ottobre 1558, nel Askeaton, County Limerick, Irlanda, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Diocese of Limerick, Munster, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmondが1504年にアイルランドムンスターで生まれた時，父Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmondは40歳，母Móur O'Brienは34歳であった。1529年にアイルランドコウントイ メアスダンボインでJoan Rocheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1542年，アイルランドコウントイ ダブリンでの職業はlord high treasurer of irelandと記録されている。彼は1558年10月27日にアイルランドコウントイ リメリックアスケアトンで54歳で死去し，アイルランドムンスターディオセゼ オフ リメリックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond(이)가 1504년 아일랜드 먼스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Móur O'Brien(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1529년 아일랜드 카운티 미스 던보이니에서 Joan Roche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1542년, 그의 직업은 아일랜드 카운티 드블린에서 lord high treasurer of ireland이었다. 그는 1558년 10월 27일 아일랜드 카운티 라임릭 아스케앳에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 먼스터 디오스스 프 라임릭에 묻혔다.

pt.from 15 June 1536 to 27 October 1558: de 15 de junho de 1536 a 27 de outubro de 1558
pt.lifeSummary: Quando James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond nasceu em 1504, em Munster, Irlanda, seu pai, Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Móur O'Brien, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Roche em 1529, em Dunboyne, County Meath, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1542, sua profissão aparece como lord high treasurer of ireland em County Dublin, Irlanda. Ele faleceu em 27 de outubro de 1558, em Askeaton, County Limerick, Irlanda, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Diocese of Limerick, Munster, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond родился в 1504 г. в местности мунстир, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Móur O'Brien, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Roche в 1529 г. в местности дунбоин, коунти мит, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1542 году он работал по профессии lord high treasurer of ireland в местности коунти дублинь, Ирландия. Он умер 27 октября 1558 г. в местности аскитон, коунти лимирикк, Ирландия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности диосес оф лимирикк, мунстир, Ирландия.

es.from 15 June 1536 to 27 October 1558: desde 15 de junio de 1536 hasta 27 de octubre de 1558
es.lifeSummary: Cuando James FitzJohn Fitzgerald, 13th Earl Desmond nació en 1504, en Munster, Irlanda, su papá, Sir John FitzThomas Fitzgerald 12th Earl of Desmond, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Móur O'Brien, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Joan Roche en 1529, en Dunboyne, County Meath, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1542, su ocupación registrada era lord high treasurer of ireland en County Dublin, Irlanda. Murió el 27 de octubre de 1558, en Askeaton, County Limerick, Irlanda, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Diocese of Limerick, Munster, Irlanda.
